Reduce engineering status meetings.
Give your team clarity automatically.

Engineering managers lose hours every week in status meetings that exist only to communicate decisions. contextprompt replaces that overhead by automatically extracting structured coding tasks from every meeting — with real file paths, implementation steps, and ownership. Your engineers leave every meeting knowing exactly what to build.

Why engineering status meetings keep multiplying

Status meetings exist because there is no reliable way to turn meeting decisions into developer tasks. contextprompt fixes that.

//

Decisions die in the meeting

Engineers leave planning and design sessions with a shared understanding. By the next day, context has faded. No one remembers which files were in scope, who owned what, or what the exact agreed-upon approach was. Another status meeting gets scheduled to re-sync.

?!

Tickets lag two days behind meetings

Manual ticket creation from meeting notes is slow, lossy, and falls to whoever volunteered to write it up. By the time the Jira ticket appears, the engineer has already started guessing at the implementation — often incorrectly.

~>

No link between discussion and code

Meeting notes say "update the auth flow." But which files? Which service? Which edge cases were discussed? Generic meeting recaps do not understand your codebase, so the engineer still has to translate every action item before they can start working.

How contextprompt reduces meeting overhead

contextprompt is the only meeting tool that scans your repositories. Every task it extracts includes the real files, functions, and implementation steps — so your engineers can start coding immediately after the meeting ends.

Repo-aware task extraction

When someone says "update the authentication middleware," contextprompt maps that to src/middleware/auth.ts in your actual codebase. Tasks reference real file paths, exported functions, and types — not generic placeholders.

Instant task availability

Within minutes of the meeting ending, structured tasks appear in your dashboard. Each task has a title, description, affected files, implementation steps, confidence level, and the exact transcript quotes that support it.

Speaker attribution and ownership

Every task is linked to who said what in the meeting. When a deadline or approach is disputed, the evidence is one click away. No more "I don't remember agreeing to that" — the transcript context is preserved with every task.

Works with your meeting platform

Paste a Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Microsoft Teams link. contextprompt sends a bot that joins as a participant, records, transcribes, and returns structured tasks — without needing a desktop app or screen recording.

Ready for AI coding assistants

The structured task format is designed to be pasted directly into Claude Code, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot. Your engineers get AI-executable task specs instead of another paragraph of meeting notes to decipher.

Eliminate the recap email

Stop spending 20 minutes after every meeting writing the recap. contextprompt produces the task list and transcript automatically. Send your team the dashboard link and let them pull what they need.

Set it up in one meeting

No integrations to configure, no plugins to install. If you have a meeting link, you can use contextprompt right now.

1

Connect your repositories

Add your GitHub repos from the dashboard. contextprompt indexes your file tree, exported functions, types, and signatures to build a codebase map. Takes about two minutes per repo.

2

Paste the meeting link

Before your next standup, sprint planning, or design review, paste the Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams link into contextprompt. The bot joins as a participant within 30 seconds.

3

Meet as normal

Run your meeting the way you always do. The bot records and transcribes with speaker identification. You do not need to take notes or change how the meeting is structured.

4

Tasks appear automatically

After the meeting, contextprompt cross-references the transcript against your codebase and extracts structured coding tasks. Share the dashboard link with your team — no manual ticket creation required.

★★★★★
“We used to hold a 30-minute sync the day after every planning session just to make sure everyone had their tasks. Now the tasks are in the dashboard before the call ends. We cancelled three recurring status meetings in the first week.”
James R. — Engineering Manager, Series B SaaS

Works across all your engineering meetings

contextprompt extracts tasks from every meeting type your team runs.

Sprint planning

Extract every story, task, and decision from sprint planning. Engineers leave with file-level implementation tasks — not vague story descriptions.

Daily standups

Log blockers, progress updates, and side-discussions as structured tasks. Keep your standup to 10 minutes and nothing falls through the cracks.

Design reviews

Architecture decisions, API contracts, and implementation constraints extracted from design discussions and mapped to real files in your codebase.

Frequently asked questions

Common questions from engineering managers evaluating contextprompt.

How does contextprompt reduce engineering status meetings?

Most status meetings exist because developers need to synchronize on what was decided and who owns what. contextprompt automates that synchronization by extracting structured tasks from every meeting automatically. When engineers can see their tasks within minutes of a meeting ending — with file paths and implementation steps — the need for a follow-up sync disappears. Teams typically cancel 2–4 recurring status meetings within the first two weeks.

What makes contextprompt different from Otter.ai or Fireflies?

Tools like Otter.ai and Fireflies produce meeting transcripts and summaries. contextprompt goes further: it scans your code repositories and maps meeting discussions to real files, functions, and implementation steps. The output is not a paragraph recap — it is a structured task that an engineer or AI coding assistant can execute immediately. See our Otter.ai comparison and Fireflies comparison.

Does my team need to change how they run meetings?

No. contextprompt joins your existing Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams calls as a bot. You paste the meeting link before the call and run the meeting exactly as you normally would. The task extraction happens automatically after the meeting ends. No templates, no structured agendas, and no behavior changes are required.

Can contextprompt handle large codebases?

Yes. contextprompt indexes your repositories with a token-aware scanner that respects .gitignore and skips node_modules, dist, and build directories. For large repos, it applies progressive trimming to stay within the analysis budget while keeping the most relevant files. You can connect multiple repos per project.

How long does task extraction take after a meeting?

Task extraction typically completes within 2–5 minutes after a meeting ends, depending on the length of the transcript. For long meetings (60+ minutes), expect up to 10 minutes for the chunked extraction and deduplication to complete. Tasks appear in your dashboard as they are processed.

Is there a free plan for engineering managers to evaluate contextprompt?

Yes. The free plan includes 1 hour of meeting recording per month with full repo-aware task extraction. No credit card is required. This is enough to run two or three real team meetings and see exactly what the output looks like for your codebase before committing to a subscription.

Your next meeting should produce tasks,
not another meeting.
